Output:
---
name: memorytree-workflow
description: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state.
---

# MemoryTree

Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application runtime feature unless the user explicitly asks to productize it.

## On Activation

When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:

1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
3. If `not-installed`: run `memorytree init` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
4. If `partial`: run `memorytree upgrade` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree daemon status` shows the heartbeat is not registered, ask the user whether they want the recommended current-repository setup via `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` or only a machine-level scheduler restore via `memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true`. Use `memorytree daemon register` only when they explicitly want custom branch, interval, report-port, or worktree settings. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.

Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.

## Core Behavior

1. Detect the repo state before changing files. Use `references/project-detection.md`.
2. If MemoryTree is missing, initialize it with `memorytree init`. If the CLI is not available, create the files from `assets/templates/`.
3. If MemoryTree is partial, upgrade it with `memorytree upgrade` before manual maintenance.
4. If MemoryTree is already installed, maintain only the active goal, bound todo, and current chat log. Use `references/update-rules.md`.
5. Keep the installation compatible with the host repo's branch, PR, CI, and review rules. Use `references/git-policy.md`.
6. Prefer safe defaults: do not add reply suffix requirements unless the host repository already requires them, do not mix code-and-memory commits by default, and do not override branch policy. MemoryTree-only git automation is allowed only inside the isolated flow in `references/git-policy.md`.
7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
11. When CLI commands are needed, use `memorytree <subcommand>` (requires Node.js ιà°?20). If Node.js is not available, fall back to manual scaffolding from `assets/templates/`. See `references/execution-environment.md`.
12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

## Read Order

1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
6. `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Detect

1. Check whether `Memory/` exists and matches the layout in `references/memory-layout.md`.
2. Classify the repo as `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`.
3. If the repo already has stronger rules than the generated templates, preserve the repo rules and only add missing MemoryTree pieces.

## Upgrade

1. Use `memorytree upgrade` for repositories that already have `AGENTS.md`, custom policy files, or partial `Memory/` content.
2. Preserve existing repo policy files by default.
3. Create only the missing MemoryTree pieces.
4. Read the upgrade result and treat `agents_merge_required=true` as a manual review task, not an overwrite instruction.

## Initialize

1. Run `memorytree init --root <repo>` and pass `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, and `--locale` when the user already provided them.
2. Use fresh init only when the repo does not already have `AGENTS.md` or another repo policy source such as a policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. If repo policy already exists, switch to `memorytree upgrade` instead of trying to skip `AGENTS.md`.
3. Review the generated `AGENTS.md` before keeping it. Merge it with the repo's existing policy if needed.
4. Keep the initial installation minimal: `Memory/01_goals`, `02_todos`, `03_chat_logs`, optional `04_knowledge`, `05_archive`, and `06_transcripts` only when transcript archival is enabled for the repo or needed for transcript import.
5. Use the locale-specific template files in `assets/templates/<locale>/` as the source of truth for first-run scaffolding.
6. Prefer `--locale auto` when the user did not request a language explicitly.
7. Use `memorytree locale` when you need a deterministic locale check without scaffolding files.
8. If Node.js is not available, create first-run files directly from `assets/templates/` instead of requiring a runtime installation.
9. If transcript archival is enabled for the repo, use `memorytree import --source <file>` for one explicit source file and `memorytree discover` when scanning local client stores. Matching transcripts must update both the repo mirror and the global archive, while unrelated projects, ambiguous ownership cases, or mismatched explicit sources are archived in the global archive only. Let the CLI generate cleaned transcripts deterministically without spending model tokens on the cleaning step.
10. Ask once whether raw transcript files may also be committed to the repository. Record the answer in `AGENTS.md`, keep the files in the repo either way, and exclude `raw` files from automatic staging until the user says yes.

## Maintain

1. Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change.
2. Update the active todo when progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer.
3. Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
4. Keep active files small. Move stale context to archive or knowledge files instead of bloating the active goal or todo.
5. Treat product ideas, architecture decisions, and operating constraints as goal or knowledge content. Treat step-by-step execution state as todo content.
6. Treat cleaned transcript indexes as discovery aids, not as the final source of truth. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript archive.
7. Prefer deterministic CLI-based transcript cleaning over manual rewriting. Do not spend model tokens to restate or normalize transcript content when `memorytree import` can do it.
8. Reply in the user's language when clear; otherwise align with repo locale without rewriting existing files just to translate them.

## Git And Safety

1. Never assume MemoryTree can override the repo's branch model.
2. Default to isolated MemoryTree-only Git operations: stage only MemoryTree-owned files, use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title, push to a dedicated branch, and open a dedicated PR.
3. Enable auto-merge only for MemoryTree-only PRs, and only when the repository still enforces its required review and CI checks.
4. Keep repo-local raw transcript mirrors and the global transcript archive synchronized on every import. The global index must remain safe for concurrent multi-project updates.
5. Keep the repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository. Unrelated or ambiguous transcripts belong in the global archive only.
6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only ιàΞ?no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

11. Do not modify the host repository's CI or E2E workflow definitions by default. If the user explicitly approves a MemoryTree-only optimization, prefer path-filtered or lightweight workflows for `Memory/**` and managed `AGENTS.md` PRs without weakening required branch protection.

## Resources

- `references/project-detection.md`: detect install state and choose init vs maintain.
- `references/memory-layout.md`: folder and file naming rules.
- `references/update-rules.md`: when to version goals, todos, and session logs.
- `references/git-policy.md`: safe Git defaults and policy merge guidance.
- `references/locale-selection.md`: locale choice rules and alias behavior.
- `references/response-language.md`: reply-language precedence and translation safety.
- `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
- `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
- `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree CLI (requires Node.js ιà°?20).
- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
- `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
- CLI commands: `memorytree init`, `memorytree upgrade`, `memorytree import`, `memorytree discover`, `memorytree locale`, `memorytree recall`, `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status|quick-start|register`.

      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Pick<\n      Automation,\n      | \"id\"\n      | \"name\"\n      | \"prompt\"\n      | \"status\"\n      | \"cwds\"\n      | \"executionEnvironment\"\n      | \"model\"\n      | \"reasoningEffort\"\n      | \"rrule\"\n    >;\n    response: {\n      item: Automation;\n    };\n  };\n  \"automation-delete\": {\n    request: Pick<Automation, \"id\">;\n    response: {\n      success: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"automation-run-now\": {\n    request: Pick<Automation, \"id\">;\n    response: {\n      success: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"list-pending-automation-run-threads\": {\n    /** Thread ids for automation runs that are pending acceptance. */\n    request: undefined;\n    response: {\n      threadIds: Array<string>;\n    };\n  };\n  \"automation-run-archive\": {\n    request: {\n      threadId: string;\n      archivedAssistantMessage?: string | null;\n      archivedUserMessage?: string | null;\n      archivedReason?: AutomationArchiveReason;\n    };\n    response: {\n      success: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"automation-run-delete\": {\n    request: {\n      threadId: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      success: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"list-pinned-threads\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: {\n      threadIds: Array<string>;\n    };\n  };\n  \"set-thread-pinned\": {\n    request: {\n      threadId: string;\n      pinned: boolean;\n    };\n    response: {\n      success: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"set-pinned-threads-order\": {\n    request: {\n      threadIds: Array<string>;\n    };\n    response: {\n      success: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"has-custom-cli-executable\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: {\n      hasCustomCliExecutable: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"find-files\": {\n    request: {\n      query: string;\n      cwd?: string | null;\n    };\n    response: {\n      files: Array<FileDescriptor>;\n    };\n  };\n  \"paths-exist\": {\n    request: {\n      paths: Array<string>;\n    };\n    response: {\n      existingPaths: Array<string>;\n    };\n  };\n  \"local-environment\": {\n    request: {\n      configPath: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      environment: LocalEnvironmentResultWithPath;\n    };\n  };\n  \"local-environments\": {\n    request: {\n      workspaceRoot: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      environments: Array<LocalEnvironmentResultWithPath>;\n    };\n  };\n  \"local-environment-config\": {\n    request: {\n      configPath: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      configPath: string;\n      exists: boolean;\n      raw: string | null;\n    };\n  };\n  \"local-environment-config-save\": {\n    request: {\n      configPath: string;\n      raw: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      configPath: string;\n      success: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n  \"ide-context\": {\n    request:\n      | {\n          // Required for Electron to determine which IDE workspace to connect to.\n          workspaceRoot: string;\n        }\n      // Not required for VS Code.\n      | undefined;\n    response: {\n      ideContext: IdeContext;\n    };\n  };\n  \"ipc-request\": {\n    request: {\n      method: keyof IpcRequestMessageContent;\n      params: IpcRequestMessageContent[keyof IpcRequestMessageContent][\"params\"];\n      targetClientId?: string;\n    };\n    response: IpcResponseMessage;\n  };\n  \"apply-patch\": {\n    request: ApplyPatchRequest & { hostConfig: HostConfig };\n    response: ApplyPatchResult;\n  };\n  \"open-in-targets\": {\n    request: { cwd: GitCwd | null };\n    response: {\n      preferredTarget?: OpenInTarget | null;\n      availableTargets: Array<OpenInTarget>;\n      targets: Array<{\n        id: OpenInTarget;\n        label: string;\n        icon: string;\n        default?: boolean;\n        available?: boolean;\n      }>;\n    };\n  };\n  \"set-preferred-app\": {\n    request: { target: OpenInTarget };\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"terminal-shell-options\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: {\n      availableShells: Array<\n        ConfigValueByKey[typeof ConfigurationKeys.INTEGRATED_TERMINAL_SHELL]\n      >;\n    };\n  };\n  \"thread-terminal-snapshot\": {\n    request: {\n      threadId: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      session: {\n        cwd: string;\n        shell: string;\n        buffer: string;\n        truncated: boolean;\n      } | null;\n    };\n  };\n  \"pick-file\": {\n    request: {\n      pickerTitle: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      file: FileDescriptor | null;\n    };\n  };\n  \"pick-files\": {\n    request: {\n      pickerTitle: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      files: Array<FileDescriptor>;\n    };\n  };\n  \"open-file\": {\n    request: {\n      /**\n       * Supported path formats (resolved against the current VS Code workspace):\n       * - Absolute filesystem path (POSIX or Windows), e.g. \"/Users/alex/src/app.ts\" or \"C:\\\\repo\\\\app.ts\".\n       * - Workspace-relative path using \"/\" or \"\\\\\", e.g. \"src/app.ts\".\n       * - Git diff paths prefixed with \"a/\" or \"b/\" (these prefixes are ignored), e.g. \"a/src/app.ts\".\n       * - Paths that include the workspace folder name as a leading segment, e.g. \"my-repo/src/app.ts\".\n       * - Bare filename or suffix path; a best-effort search will open the first match, e.g. \"app.ts\" or \"src/app.ts\".\n       *\n       * Notes:\n       * - This field expects a filesystem path string, not a URI. Schemes like \"file://\", \"vscode://\", or \"https://\" are not accepted.\n       */\n      path: string;\n      /** Optional preferred target; when omitted, the host chooses the preferred target. */\n      target?: OpenInTarget | null;\n      /** 1-based line number to select (optional). */\n      line?: number;\n      /** 1-based column number to select (optional, defaults to 1). */\n      column?: number;\n      /** Base directory to resolve relative paths against; pass null when unavailable. */\n      cwd: GitCwd | null;\n      /**\n       * When this and target are provided, this target will be persisted as the preferred target for this path.\n       */\n      persistPreferredTargetPath?: string;\n    };\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"git-origins\": {\n    request:\n      | undefined\n      | {\n          /** Optional host id to resolve git metadata against. */\n          hostId?: string;\n          /**\n           * Optional list of directories to resolve git origins for.\n           * When omitted, the workspace root options are used.\n           */\n          dirs?: Array<string>;\n        };\n    response: {\n      origins: Array<{\n        /**\n         * If dirs were requested, this will be the directory that was requested.\n         * If not, it will be one of the workspace roots.\n         * Either is either the same or a subdirectory of the root.\n         */\n        dir: string;\n        root: GitRoot;\n        originUrl: string | null;\n        commonDir?: string | null;\n      }>;\n      homeDir: string;\n    };\n  };\n  \"git-push\": {\n    request: GitPushRequest;\n    response: GitPushResponse;\n  };\n  \"git-create-branch\": {\n    request: GitCreateBranchRequest;\n    response: GitCreateBranchResponse;\n  };\n  \"git-checkout-branch\": {\n    request: GitCheckoutBranchRequest;\n    response: GitCheckoutBranchResponse;\n  };\n  \"gh-cli-status\": {\n    request:\n      | undefined\n      | {\n          hostId?: string;\n        };\n    response: GhCliStatus;\n  };\n  \"gh-pr-create\": {\n    request: GhCreatePullRequestRequest;\n    response: GhCreatePullRequestResponse;\n  };\n  \"gh-pr-board\": {\n    request: GhPullRequestBoardRequest;\n    response: GhPullRequestBoardResponse;\n  };\n  \"gh-pr-status\": {\n    request: GhPullRequestStatusRequest;\n    response: GhPullRequestStatusResponse;\n  };\n  \"gh-pr-merge\": {\n    request: GhMergePullRequestRequest;\n    response: GhMergePullRequestResponse;\n  };\n  /** Returns the merge-base commit hash between HEAD and the provided base branch. */\n  \"git-merge-base\": {\n    request: {\n      gitRoot?: string;\n      baseBranch: string;\n      hostId?: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      mergeBaseSha: string | null;\n    };\n  };\n  \"prepare-worktree-snapshot\": {\n    request: {\n      gitRoot: string;\n      snapshotBranch: string | null;\n    };\n    response: {\n      tarballPath: string;\n      tarballFilename: string;\n      tarballSize: number;\n      repoName: string;\n      branch: string | null;\n      snapshotBranch: string;\n      commitSha: string;\n      remotes: Record<string, string>;\n      contentType: string;\n    };\n  };\n  \"upload-worktree-snapshot\": {\n    request: {\n      tarballPath: string;\n      uploadUrl: string;\n      contentLength: number;\n      contentType: string;\n    };\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"confirm-trace-recording-start\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"cancel-trace-recording-start\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"submit-trace-recording-details\": {\n    request: {\n      note: string;\n      conversationId: string | null;\n    };\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"get-configuration\": {\n    request: {\n      key: (typeof ConfigurationKeys)[keyof typeof ConfigurationKeys];\n    };\n    response: {\n      value: unknown;\n    };\n  };\n  \"set-configuration\": {\n    request: {\n      key: ConfigurationKey;\n      value: ConfigValueByKey[ConfigurationKey];\n    };\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"get-global-state\": {\n    request: {\n      key: (typeof GlobalStateKey)[keyof typeof GlobalStateKey];\n    };\n    response: {\n      value: unknown;\n    };\n  };\n  \"set-global-state\": {\n    request: {\n      key: (typeof GlobalStateKey)[keyof typeof GlobalStateKey];\n      value: unknown;\n    };\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  /**\n   * Set a VS Code context key in the workbench. Useful for keybindings/menus.\n   */\n  \"set-vs-context\": {\n    request: {\n      key: string;\n      value?: string | boolean;\n    };\n    response: { success: boolean };\n  };\n  \"get-copilot-api-proxy-info\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: {\n      baseUrl: string;\n      /** Value of the API key. */\n      secret: string;\n    } | null;\n  };\n  \"mcp-codex-config\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: {\n      config: CodexConfig | null;\n    };\n  };\n  \"worktree-shell-environment-config\": {\n    request: {\n      cwd: string;\n    };\n    response: {\n      shellEnvironment: {\n        version: 1;\n        set: Record<string, string>;\n        exclude: Array<string>;\n      } | null;\n    };\n  };\n  \"is-copilot-api-available\": {\n    request: undefined;\n    response: {\n      available: boolean;\n    };\n  };\n};\n\n// This enforces that all of the VSCodeFetchRequest and VSCodeMutationRequest keys have a request and response.\ntype AssertSubtype<_Sub extends Super, Super> = true;\ntype BaseVSCodeFetchRequest = {\n  request: unknown;\n  response: unknown;\n};\ntype _AssertAllVSCodeRequestsHaveRequestAndResponse = AssertSubtype<\n  VSCodeFetchRequest,\n  Record<string, BaseVSCodeFetchRequest>\n>;\ntype _AssertHotkeyWindowMutationStateMatchesQueryResponse = AssertSubtype<\n  HotkeyWindowHotkeyMutationResponse[\"state\"],\n  VSCodeFetchRequest[\"hotkey-window-hotkey-state\"][\"response\"]\n>;\ndeclare const _useAssertRequest: _AssertAllVSCodeRequestsHaveRequestAndResponse;\ndeclare const _useAssertHotkeyWindowMutationStateMatchesQueryResponse: _AssertHotkeyWindowMutationStateMatchesQueryResponse;\n","import type { ConversationId } from \"./protocol\";\n\nexport const HOTKEY_WINDOW_ROUTE_PREFIX = \"/hotkey-window\";\nexport const HOTKEY_WINDOW_HOME_ROUTE = HOTKEY_WINDOW_ROUTE_PREFIX;\nconst HOTKEY_WINDOW_THREAD_ROUTE_PREFIX = `${HOTKEY_WINDOW_ROUTE_PREFIX}/thread`;\nexport const HOTKEY_WINDOW_THREAD_ROUTE_PATTERN = `${HOTKEY_WINDOW_THREAD_ROUTE_PREFIX}/:conversationId`;\nconst HOTKEY_WINDOW_REMOTE_ROUTE_PREFIX = `${HOTKEY_WINDOW_ROUTE_PREFIX}/remote`;\nexport const HOTKEY_WINDOW_REMOTE_ROUTE_PATTERN = `${HOTKEY_WINDOW_REMOTE_ROUTE_PREFIX}/:taskId`;\nconst HOTKEY_WINDOW_WORKTREE_INIT_ROUTE_PREFIX = `${HOTKEY_WINDOW_ROUTE_PREFIX}/worktree-init-v2`;\nexport const HOTKEY_WINDOW_WORKTREE_INIT_ROUTE_PATTERN = `${HOTKEY_WINDOW_WORKTREE_INIT_ROUTE_PREFIX}/:pendingWorktreeId`;\n\nconst LOCAL_CONVERSATION_ROUTE_PREFIX = \"/local\";\nexport const LOCAL_CONVERSATION_ROUTE_PATTERN = {
	"name": "chatgpt",
	"publisher": "openai",
	"version": "26.323.20928",
	"displayName": "Codex ιàΞ?OpenAIιàΞφΣö coding agent",
	"description": "Codex is a coding agent that works with you everywhere you code ιàΞ?included in ChatGPT Plus, Pro, Business, Edu, and Enterprise plans.",
	"license": "SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE.md",
	"engines": {
		"vscode": "^1.96.2"
	},
	"categories": [
		"AI",
		"Chat"
	],
	"keywords": [
		"codex",
		"openai",
		"chatgpt",
		"ai",
		"agent",
		"coding",
		"code",
		"chat"
	],
	"activationEvents": [
		"onStartupFinished",
		"onUri"
	],
	"main": "./out/extension.js",
	"enabledApiProposals": [
		"chatSessionsProvider",
		"languageModelProxy"
	],
	"icon": "resources/blossom.dark.png",
	"contributes": {
		"keybindings": [
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.newChat",
				"key": "ctrl+n",
				"mac": "cmd+n",
				"when": "chatgpt.supportsNewChatKeyShortcut"
			}
		],
		"commands": [
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.implementTodo",
				"title": "Implement with Codex",
				"category": "Codex",
				"enablement": "false"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.openSidebar",
				"title": "Open Codex Sidebar",
				"category": "Codex",
				"icon": {
					"light": "resources/blossom-black.svg",
					"dark": "resources/blossom-white.svg"
				}
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.openCommandMenu",
				"title": "Open Codex Command Menu",
				"category": "Codex"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.newCodexPanel",
				"title": "New Codex Agent",
				"category": "Codex",
				"icon": "$(plus)"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.addToThread",
				"title": "Add to Codex Thread",
				"category": "Codex"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.addFileToThread",
				"title": "Add File to Codex Thread",
				"category": "Codex"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.newChat",
				"title": "New Thread in Codex Sidebar",
				"category": "Codex"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.showLspMcpCliArgs",
				"title": "Copy Codex CLI args for LSP MCP",
				"category": "Codex"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.dumpNuxState",
				"title": "Debug: print NUX state to console",
				"category": "Codex",
				"enablement": "chatgpt.sidebarView.visible"
			},
			{
				"command": "chatgpt.resetNuxState",
				"title": "Debug: reset NUX state",
				"category": "Codex",
				"enablement": "chatgpt.sidebarView.visible"
			}
		],
		"configuration": {
			"type": "object",
			"title": "Codex Settings",
			"properties": {
				"chatgpt.commentCodeLensEnabled": {
					"description": "Enable CodeLens above TODO comments to implement with Codex.",
					"type": "boolean",
					"default": true
				},
				"chatgpt.cliExecutable": {
					"description": "DEVELOPMENT ONLY: Path to the Codex CLI executable. You do NOT need to set this unless you are actively developing the Codex CLI. If set this manually, parts of the extension may not work as expected.",
					"type": [
						"string",
						"null"
					],
					"default": null,
					"scope": "application",
					"restricted": true
				},
				"chatgpt.openOnStartup": {
					"description": "Focus the Codex sidebar when the extension finishes starting up.",
					"type": "boolean",
					"default": false
				},
				"chatgpt.followUpQueueMode": {
					"description": "Control whether follow-up messages are queued or steer the current run. Press Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+Enter to do the opposite for a single in-progress follow-up.",
					"type": "string",
					"enum": [
						"queue",
						"steer",
						"interrupt"
					],
					"default": "queue"
				},
				"chatgpt.composerEnterBehavior": {
					"description": "Enter behavior for the Codex composer.",
					"type": "string",
					"enum": [
						"enter",
						"cmdIfMultiline"
					],
					"default": "enter"
				},
				"chatgpt.localeOverride": {
					"description": "Preferred language for the Codex UI. Leave empty to auto detect.",
					"type": [
						"string",
						"null"
					],
					"default": null,
					"scope": "application"
				},
				"chatgpt.runCodexInWindowsSubsystemForLinux": {
					"description": "Windows only: when Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) is installed, automatically run Codex inside WSL. Recommended for improved sandbox security and better performance - Agent mode on Windows currently requires WSL. Changing this setting reloads VS Code to take effect.",
					"type": "boolean",
					"default": false
				}
			}
		},
		"viewsContainers": {
			"activitybar": [
				{
					"id": "codexViewContainer",
					"title": "Codex",
					"icon": "resources/blossom-white.svg",
					"when": "chatgpt.doesNotSupportSecondarySidebar"
				}
			],
			"secondarySidebar": [
				{
					"id": "codexSecondaryViewContainer",
					"title": "Codex",
					"icon": "resources/blossom-white.svg",
					"when": "!chatgpt.doesNotSupportSecondarySidebar"
				}
			]
		},
		"views": {
			"codexViewContainer": [
				{
					"id": "chatgpt.sidebarView",
					"type": "webview",
					"name": "Codex",
					"when": "chatgpt.doesNotSupportSecondarySidebar"
				}
			],
			"codexSecondaryViewContainer": [
				{
					"id": "chatgpt.sidebarSecondaryView",
					"type": "webview",
					"name": "Codex",
					"when": "!chatgpt.doesNotSupportSecondarySidebar"
				}
			]
		},
		"customEditors": [
			{
				"viewType": "chatgpt.conversationEditor",
				"displayName": "Codex Task",
				"priority": "default",
				"selector": [
					{
						"filenamePattern": "openai-codex:/**/*"
					}
				]
			}
		],
		"menus": {
			"editor/title": [
				{
					"command": "chatgpt.openSidebar",
					"group": "navigation"
				}
			],
			"editor/context": [
				{
					"command": "chatgpt.addToThread",
					"group": "codex",
					"when": "resourceScheme == file"
				}
			],
			"editor/title/context": [
				{
					"command": "chatgpt.addFileToThread",
					"group": "2_chat",
					"order": 2,
					"when": "resourceScheme == file"
				}
			],
			"webview/context": [
				{
					"command": "chatgpt.newChat",
					"when": "(webviewId == 'chatgpt.sidebarView' || webviewId == 'chatgpt.sidebarSecondaryView') && chatgpt.supportsNewChatMenu"
				}
			],
			"commandPalette": [
				{
					"command": "chatgpt.showLspMcpCliArgs",
					"when": "chatgpt.lspMcpEnabled"
				}
			],
			"chatSessions/newSession": [
				{
					"command": "chatgpt.newCodexPanel",
					"when": "chatSessionType == openai-codex"
				}
			]
		},
		"chatSessions": [
			{
				"type": "openai-codex",
				"name": "Codex",
				"displayName": "OpenAI Codex",
				"description": "OpenAI Codex integration for VS Code"
			}
		],
		"grammars": [
			{
				"language": "codex-rules",
				"scopeName": "source.codex-rules",
				"path": "./syntaxes/starlark.tmLanguage.json"
			}
		],
		"languages": [
			{
				"id": "codex-rules",
				"aliases": [
					"Codex Rules"
				],
				"extensions": [
					".rules"
				],
				"configuration": "./syntaxes/starlark.configuration.json"
			}
		]
	},
	"__metadata": {
		"installedTimestamp": 1774387609397,
		"targetPlatform": "win32-x64",
		"size": 489689280
	}
}

{
  "turn_id": "019d2381-4112-71c2-a836-cd95badc0c44",
  "cwd": "d:\\demo1\\openmnemo",
  "current_date": "2026-03-25",
  "timezone": "Asia/Shanghai",
  "approval_policy": "never",
  "sandbox_policy": {
    "type": "danger-full-access"
  },
  "model": "gpt-5.4",
  "personality": "friendly",
  "collaboration_mode": {
    "mode": "default",
    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

# Project Todo

## Binding

## Current Focus
Stage gate confirmed on 2026-03-25: prioritize foundation work that makes memory invocation stable, precise, replayable, and traceable. Do not let super-agent orchestration displace this bottom-layer work before the substrate is proven.

## Execution Notes
- Newly confirmed product rule: any capability that does not directly improve memory-call correctness, stability, traceability, or replayability should not move ahead of the substrate. Agent orchestration remains an upper-layer follow-on after the memory layer is dependable.

Output:
---
name: memorytree-workflow
description: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state.
---

# MemoryTree

Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application runtime feature unless the user explicitly asks to productize it.

## On Activation

When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:

1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
3. If `not-installed`: run `memorytree init` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
4. If `partial`: run `memorytree upgrade` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree daemon status` shows the heartbeat is not registered, ask the user whether they want the recommended current-repository setup via `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` or only a machine-level scheduler restore via `memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true`. Use `memorytree daemon register` only when they explicitly want custom branch, interval, report-port, or worktree settings. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.

Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.

## Core Behavior

1. Detect the repo state before changing files. Use `references/project-detection.md`.
2. If MemoryTree is missing, initialize it with `memorytree init`. If the CLI is not available, create the files from `assets/templates/`.
3. If MemoryTree is partial, upgrade it with `memorytree upgrade` before manual maintenance.
4. If MemoryTree is already installed, maintain only the active goal, bound todo, and current chat log. Use `references/update-rules.md`.
5. Keep the installation compatible with the host repo's branch, PR, CI, and review rules. Use `references/git-policy.md`.
6. Prefer safe defaults: do not add reply suffix requirements unless the host repository already requires them, do not mix code-and-memory commits by default, and do not override branch policy. MemoryTree-only git automation is allowed only inside the isolated flow in `references/git-policy.md`.
7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
11. When CLI commands are needed, use `memorytree <subcommand>` (requires Node.js ιà°?20). If Node.js is not available, fall back to manual scaffolding from `assets/templates/`. See `references/execution-environment.md`.
12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

## Read Order

1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
6. `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Detect

1. Check whether `Memory/` exists and matches the layout in `references/memory-layout.md`.
2. Classify the repo as `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`.
3. If the repo already has stronger rules than the generated templates, preserve the repo rules and only add missing MemoryTree pieces.

## Upgrade

1. Use `memorytree upgrade` for repositories that already have `AGENTS.md`, custom policy files, or partial `Memory/` content.
2. Preserve existing repo policy files by default.
3. Create only the missing MemoryTree pieces.
4. Read the upgrade result and treat `agents_merge_required=true` as a manual review task, not an overwrite instruction.

## Initialize

1. Run `memorytree init --root <repo>` and pass `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, and `--locale` when the user already provided them.
2. Use fresh init only when the repo does not already have `AGENTS.md` or another repo policy source such as a policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. If repo policy already exists, switch to `memorytree upgrade` instead of trying to skip `AGENTS.md`.
3. Review the generated `AGENTS.md` before keeping it. Merge it with the repo's existing policy if needed.
4. Keep the initial installation minimal: `Memory/01_goals`, `02_todos`, `03_chat_logs`, optional `04_knowledge`, `05_archive`, and `06_transcripts` only when transcript archival is enabled for the repo or needed for transcript import.
5. Use the locale-specific template files in `assets/templates/<locale>/` as the source of truth for first-run scaffolding.
6. Prefer `--locale auto` when the user did not request a language explicitly.
7. Use `memorytree locale` when you need a deterministic locale check without scaffolding files.
8. If Node.js is not available, create first-run files directly from `assets/templates/` instead of requiring a runtime installation.
9. If transcript archival is enabled for the repo, use `memorytree import --source <file>` for one explicit source file and `memorytree discover` when scanning local client stores. Matching transcripts must update both the repo mirror and the global archive, while unrelated projects, ambiguous ownership cases, or mismatched explicit sources are archived in the global archive only. Let the CLI generate cleaned transcripts deterministically without spending model tokens on the cleaning step.
10. Ask once whether raw transcript files may also be committed to the repository. Record the answer in `AGENTS.md`, keep the files in the repo either way, and exclude `raw` files from automatic staging until the user says yes.

## Maintain

1. Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change.
2. Update the active todo when progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer.
3. Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
4. Keep active files small. Move stale context to archive or knowledge files instead of bloating the active goal or todo.
5. Treat product ideas, architecture decisions, and operating constraints as goal or knowledge content. Treat step-by-step execution state as todo content.
6. Treat cleaned transcript indexes as discovery aids, not as the final source of truth. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript archive.
7. Prefer deterministic CLI-based transcript cleaning over manual rewriting. Do not spend model tokens to restate or normalize transcript content when `memorytree import` can do it.
8. Reply in the user's language when clear; otherwise align with repo locale without rewriting existing files just to translate them.

## Git And Safety

1. Never assume MemoryTree can override the repo's branch model.
2. Default to isolated MemoryTree-only Git operations: stage only MemoryTree-owned files, use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title, push to a dedicated branch, and open a dedicated PR.
3. Enable auto-merge only for MemoryTree-only PRs, and only when the repository still enforces its required review and CI checks.
4. Keep repo-local raw transcript mirrors and the global transcript archive synchronized on every import. The global index must remain safe for concurrent multi-project updates.
5. Keep the repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository. Unrelated or ambiguous transcripts belong in the global archive only.
6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only ιàΞ?no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

11. Do not modify the host repository's CI or E2E workflow definitions by default. If the user explicitly approves a MemoryTree-only optimization, prefer path-filtered or lightweight workflows for `Memory/**` and managed `AGENTS.md` PRs without weakening required branch protection.

## Resources

- `references/project-detection.md`: detect install state and choose init vs maintain.
- `references/memory-layout.md`: folder and file naming rules.
- `references/update-rules.md`: when to version goals, todos, and session logs.
- `references/git-policy.md`: safe Git defaults and policy merge guidance.
- `references/locale-selection.md`: locale choice rules and alias behavior.
- `references/response-language.md`: reply-language precedence and translation safety.
- `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
- `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
- `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree CLI (requires Node.js ιà°?20).
- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
- `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
- CLI commands: `memorytree init`, `memorytree upgrade`, `memorytree import`, `memorytree discover`, `memorytree locale`, `memorytree recall`, `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status|quick-start|register`.

{
  "turn_id": "019d239c-2893-7293-ab29-cc4b5c279c0a",
  "cwd": "d:\\demo1\\openmnemo",
  "current_date": "2026-03-25",
  "timezone": "Asia/Shanghai",
  "approval_policy": "never",
  "sandbox_policy": {
    "type": "danger-full-access"
  },
  "model": "gpt-5.4",
  "personality": "friendly",
  "collaboration_mode": {
    "mode": "default",
    "settings": {
      "model": "gpt-5.4",
      "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

}\n\n  const nestedValue = Object.getOwnPropertyDescriptor(features, key)?.value;\n  return typeof nestedValue === \"boolean\" ? nestedValue : undefined;\n}\n\nconst AGENT_MODE_REQUIREMENTS: Record<\n  NonCustomAgentMode,\n  {\n    sandboxMode: AppServer.v2.SandboxMode;\n    approvalPolicy: AppServer.v2.AskForApproval;\n  }\n> = {\n  \"read-only\": {\n    sandboxMode: \"read-only\",\n    approvalPolicy: \"on-request\",\n  },\n  auto: {\n    sandboxMode: \"workspace-write\",\n    approvalPolicy: \"on-request\",\n  },\n  \"guardian-approvals\": {\n    sandboxMode: \"workspace-write\",\n    approvalPolicy: \"on-request\",\n  },\n  \"full-access\": {\n    sandboxMode: \"danger-full-access\",\n    approvalPolicy: \"never\",\n  },\n};\n\nexport function getSandboxModeForPolicy(\n  sandboxPolicy: AppServer.v2.SandboxPolicy,\n): AppServer.v2.SandboxMode | null {\n  switch (sandboxPolicy.type) {\n    case \"dangerFullAccess\":\n      return \"danger-full-access\";\n    case \"readOnly\":\n      return \"read-only\";\n    case \"workspaceWrite\":\n      return \"workspace-write\";\n    case \"externalSandbox\":\n      return null;\n  }\n}\n\n// Permission validation\nexport function resolveAvailableAgentModes(\n  requirements: AppServer.v2.ConfigRequirements | null,\n  userSavedConfig?: AppServer.v2.Config | null,\n): Array<AgentMode> {\n  const isCustomModeEnabled =\n    !!userSavedConfig?.approval_policy || !!userSavedConfig?.sandbox_mode;\n  const agentModes = resolveAgentModesFromRequirements(requirements);\n  const customModeWithOnlySandboxAndApproval = isCustomModeEnabled\n    ? buildCustomPermissionConfig([], userSavedConfig)\n    : null;\n  const canShowCustom =\n    customModeWithOnlySandboxAndApproval &&\n    isPolicyAllowedByRequirements(\n      requirements,\n      getSandboxModeForPolicy(\n        customModeWithOnlySandboxAndApproval.sandboxPolicy,\n      ),\n      customModeWithOnlySandboxAndApproval.approvalPolicy,\n    );\n  if (canShowCustom) {\n    return [...agentModes, \"custom\"];\n  }\n  return agentModes;\n}\n\nexport function resolveAvailableNonFullAccessAgentMode(\n  availableAgentModes: Array<AgentMode>,\n): NonFullAccessDropdownMode {\n  for (const mode of AGENT_MODE_PRIORITY_LIST) {\n    if (availableAgentModes.includes(mode)) {\n      return mode;\n    }\n  }\n  return DEFAULT_NON_FULL_ACCESS_AGENT_MODE;\n}\n\nfunction isPolicyAllowedByRequirements(\n  requirements: AppServer.v2.ConfigRequirements | null,\n  sandboxMode: AppServer.v2.SandboxMode | null,\n  approvalPolicy: AppServer.v2.AskForApproval | null,\n): boolean {\n  if (requirements == null) {\n    return true;\n  }\n  const allowedSandboxModes = requirements.allowedSandboxModes;\n  if (\n    allowedSandboxModes != null &&\n    sandboxMode &&\n    !allowedSandboxModes.includes(sandboxMode)\n  ) {\n    return false;\n  }\n  const allowedApprovalPolicies = requirements.allowedApprovalPolicies;\n  if (\n    allowedApprovalPolicies != null &&\n    approvalPolicy &&\n    !allowedApprovalPolicies.includes(approvalPolicy)\n  ) {\n    return false;\n  }\n  return true;\n}\n\nexport function resolveAgentModesFromRequirements(\n  requirements: AppServer.v2.ConfigRequirements | null,\n): Array<AgentMode> {\n  return BUILT_IN_AGENT_MODE_LIST.filter((mode) =>\n    isAgentModeAllowedByRequirements(mode, requirements),\n  );\n}\n\nexport function isAgentModeAllowedByRequirements(\n  mode: Exclude<AgentMode, \"custom\">,\n  requirements: AppServer.v2.ConfigRequirements | null,\n): boolean {\n  if (requirements == null) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const { sandboxMode, approvalPolicy } = AGENT_MODE_REQUIREMENTS[mode];\n  return isPolicyAllowedByRequirements(\n    requirements,\n    sandboxMode,\n    approvalPolicy,\n  );\n}\n\n// Permission build\nexport function buildWorkspaceWritePermissionsConfig(\n  writableRoots: Array<string>,\n  sandboxWorkspaceWrite?: AppServer.v2.SandboxWorkspaceWrite | null,\n  approvalPolicy?: AppServer.v2.AskForApproval | null,\n  approvalsReviewer: AppServer.v2.ApprovalsReviewer = APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER,\n): PermissionsConfig {\n  return {\n    sandboxPolicy: {\n      type: \"workspaceWrite\",\n      writableRoots: [\n        ...writableRoots,\n        ...(sandboxWorkspaceWrite?.writable_roots || []),\n      ],\n      readOnlyAccess: FULL_READ_ONLY_ACCESS,\n      excludeSlashTmp: sandboxWorkspaceWrite?.exclude_slash_tmp ?? false,\n      excludeTmpdirEnvVar:\n        sandboxWorkspaceWrite?.exclude_tmpdir_env_var ?? false,\n      networkAccess: sandboxWorkspaceWrite?.network_access ?? false,\n    },\n    approvalPolicy: approvalPolicy ?? \"on-request\",\n    approvalsReviewer,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction buildReadOnlyPermissionsConfig(\n  approvalPolicy?: AppServer.v2.AskForApproval | null,\n  approvalsReviewer: AppServer.v2.ApprovalsReviewer = APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER,\n): PermissionsConfig {\n  return {\n    sandboxPolicy: READ_ONLY_SANDBOX_POLICY,\n    approvalPolicy: approvalPolicy ?? \"on-request\",\n    approvalsReviewer,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction buildFullAccessPermissionsConfig(\n  approvalPolicy?: AppServer.v2.AskForApproval | null,\n  approvalsReviewer: AppServer.v2.ApprovalsReviewer = APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER,\n): PermissionsConfig {\n  return {\n    sandboxPolicy: { type: \"dangerFullAccess\" },\n    approvalPolicy: approvalPolicy ?? \"never\",\n    approvalsReviewer,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction buildCustomPermissionConfig(\n  writableRoots: Array<string>,\n  config: AppServer.v2.Config,\n): PermissionsConfig {\n  const approvalsReviewer = getApprovalsReviewerFromUserConfig(config);\n  switch (config.sandbox_mode) {\n    case \"danger-full-access\":\n      return buildFullAccessPermissionsConfig(\n        config.approval_policy,\n        approvalsReviewer,\n      );\n    case \"read-only\":\n      return buildReadOnlyPermissionsConfig(\n        config.approval_policy,\n        approvalsReviewer,\n      );\n    case \"workspace-write\":\n      return buildWorkspaceWritePermissionsConfig(\n        writableRoots,\n        config.sandbox_workspace_write,\n        config.approval_policy,\n        approvalsReviewer,\n      );\n    case null:\n    case undefined:\n      return buildReadOnlyPermissionsConfig(\n        config.approval_policy,\n        approvalsReviewer,\n      );\n  }\n}\n\nexport function buildPermissionsConfigForMode(\n  mode: AgentMode,\n  writableRoots: Array<string>,\n  config: AppServer.v2.Config,\n): PermissionsConfig {\n  switch (mode) {\n    case \"read-only\":\n      return buildReadOnlyPermissionsConfig();\n    case \"full-access\":\n      return buildFullAccessPermissionsConfig();\n    case \"auto\":\n      return buildWorkspaceWritePermissionsConfig(writableRoots);\n    case \"guardian-approvals\":\n      return buildWorkspaceWritePermissionsConfig(\n        writableRoots,\n        undefined,\n        undefined,\n        APPROVALS_REVIEWER_GUARDIAN_SUBAGENT,\n      );\n    case \"custom\":\n      return buildCustomPermissionConfig(writableRoots, config);\n  }\n}\n\nexport function getApprovalsReviewerFromUserConfig(\n  config?: AppServer.v2.Config,\n): AppServer.v2.ApprovalsReviewer {\n  const value = config?.[APPROVALS_REVIEWER_CONFIG_KEY];\n  if (\n    value !== APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER &&\n    value !== APPROVALS_REVIEWER_GUARDIAN_SUBAGENT\n  ) {\n    return APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER;\n  }\n\n  if (\n    value === APPROVALS_REVIEWER_GUARDIAN_SUBAGENT &&\n    readGuardianApprovalFlag(config) === false\n  ) {\n    return APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER;\n  }\n\n  return value;\n}\n\nfunction usesDefaultWorkspaceWriteSettings(\n  sandboxWorkspaceWrite?: AppServer.v2.SandboxWorkspaceWrite | null,\n): boolean {\n  if (sandboxWorkspaceWrite == null) {\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return (\n    (sandboxWorkspaceWrite.writable_roots?.length ?? 0) === 0 &&\n    (sandboxWorkspaceWrite.network_access ?? false) === false &&\n    (sandboxWorkspaceWrite.exclude_slash_tmp ?? false) === false &&\n    (sandboxWorkspaceWrite.exclude_tmpdir_env_var ?? false) === false\n  );\n}\n\nexport function getBuiltInAgentModeForConfig(\n  config?: AppServer.v2.Config,\n  workspaceWriteMode: WorkspaceWriteAgentMode = \"auto\",\n): NonCustomAgentMode | null {\n  const sandboxMode = config?.sandbox_mode ?? null;\n  const approvalPolicy = config?.approval_policy ?? null;\n  const sandboxWorkspaceWrite = config?.sandbox_workspace_write;\n  const isUnsetConfig =\n    sandboxMode == null &&\n    approvalPolicy == null &&\n    sandboxWorkspaceWrite == null;\n  const matchesOnRequestOrUnset =\n    approvalPolicy === \"on-request\" || approvalPolicy == null;\n  const matchesNeverOrUnset =\n    approvalPolicy === \"never\" || approvalPolicy == null;\n  const usesDefaultWorkspaceWriteDefaults = usesDefaultWorkspaceWriteSettings(\n    sandboxWorkspaceWrite,\n  );\n\n  if (isUnsetConfig) {\n    return workspaceWriteMode;\n  }\n\n  if (\n    (sandboxMode === \"read-only\" || sandboxMode == null) &&\n    matchesOnRequestOrUnset\n  ) {\n    return \"read-only\";\n  }\n\n  if (\n    sandboxMode === \"workspace-write\" &&\n    matchesOnRequestOrUnset &&\n    usesDefaultWorkspaceWriteDefaults\n  ) {\n    return workspaceWriteMode;\n  }\n\n  if (sandboxMode === \"danger-full-access\" && matchesNeverOrUnset) {\n    return \"full-access\";\n  }\n\n  return null;\n}\n\nexport function readGuardianApprovalFlag(\n  config?: AppServer.v2.Config,\n): boolean | undefined {\n  return readBooleanFeatureFlag(config, GUARDIAN_APPROVAL_FEATURE_NAME);\n}\n","import type * as AppServer from \"app-server-types\";\n\nimport type { PermissionsConfig } from \"./permissions-config\";\nimport {\n  APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER,\n  getSandboxModeForPolicy,\n} from \"./permissions-config\";\nimport type { JsonValue } from \"./types/json-value\";\n\nexport type CodexConfig = { [key in string]?: JsonValue };\nexport type FetchCodexConfig = () => Promise<CodexConfig | null>;\n\nexport const COPILOT_MODEL_PROVIDER_ID = \"codex_vscode_copilot\";\nexport const COPILOT_MODEL_PROVIDER_PRESENTATION_NAME = \"Copilot\";\n\n/**\n * `workspaceRoots` must contain at least one entry or `cwd` will be invalid.\n *\n * Further, `workspaceRoots` should be derived from `getActiveWorkspaceRoots()` or\n * the `\"active-workspace-roots\"` fetch handler to ensure proper WSL path conversion.\n */\nexport async function buildNewConversationParams(\n  model: string | null,\n  serviceTier: AppServer.ServiceTier | null | undefined,\n  fetchProxyConfig: () => Promise<{\n    baseUrl: string;\n    /** Value of the API key. */\n    secret: string;\n  } | null>,\n  cwd: string,\n  perms: PermissionsConfig,\n  fetchCodexConfig?: FetchCodexConfig,\n  personality?: AppServer.Personality | null,\n  approvalsReviewer: AppServer.v2.ApprovalsReviewer = APPROVALS_REVIEWER_USER,\n): Promise<AppServer.v2.ThreadStartParams> {\n  const config: { [key in string]?: JsonValue } = {};\n  const proxyConfig = await fetchProxyConfig();\n  let modelProvider: string | null;\n  if (proxyConfig != null) {\n    modelProvider = COPILOT_MODEL_PROVIDER_ID;\n    const { baseUrl, secret } = proxyConfig;\n    config.model_provider = COPILOT_MODEL_PROVIDER_ID;\n    config[`model_providers.${COPILOT_MODEL_PROVIDER_ID}`] = {\n      name: COPILOT_MODEL_PROVIDER_PRESENTATION_NAME,\n      base_url: baseUrl,\n      experimental_bearer_token: secret,\n      wire_api: \"responses\",\n    };\n  } else {\n    modelProvider = null;\n  }\n\n  if (fetchCodexConfig) {\n    const codexConfig = await fetchCodexConfig();\n    if (codexConfig) {\n      for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(codexConfig)) {\n        config[key] = value;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return {\n    cwd,\n    model,\n    modelProvider,\n    ...(serviceTier !== undefined ? { serviceTier } : {}),\n    approvalsReviewer,\n    config,\n    approvalPolicy: perms.approvalPolicy,\n    baseInstructions: null,\n    developerInstructions: null,\n    sandbox: getSandboxModeForPolicy(perms.sandboxPolicy),\n    personality: personality ?? null,\n    ephemeral: null,\n    mockExperimentalField: null,\n    experimentalRawEvents: false,\n    dynamicTools: null,\n    persistExtendedHistory: false,\n  };\n}\n","export type RepoInfo = { host: string; owner: string; repo: string };\n\ntype ParseResult<T> = { success: true; value: T } | { success: false };\n\nexport function parseRepoFromRemoteUrl(remoteUrl: string): RepoInfo | null {\n  const trimmedRemoteUrl = remoteUrl.trim();\n  if (!trimmedRemoteUrl) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  if (/^[a-zA-Z]:[\\\\/]/.test(trimmedRemoteUrl)) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  if (trimmedRemoteUrl.includes(\"://\")) {\n    const parsedFromUrlStyle = parseRepoFromUrlStyleRemoteUrl(trimmedRemoteUrl);\n    return parsedFromUrlStyle.success ? parsedFromUrlStyle.value : null;\n  }\n\n  const parsedFromScpStyle = parseRepoFromScpStyleRemoteUrl(trimmedRemoteUrl);\n  return parsedFromScpStyle.success ? parsedFromScpStyle.value : null;\n}\n\nexport function parseRepoFromUrlStyleRemoteUrl(\n  remoteUrl: string,\n): ParseResult<RepoInfo> {\n  try {\n    const parsedUrl = new URL(remoteUrl);\n    const host = parsedUrl.hostname;\n    if (!host) {\n      return { success: false };\n    }\n\n    const normalizedPath = parsedUrl.pathname\n      .replace(/^\\/+/, \"\")\n      .replace(/\\.git$/i, \"\");\n    const parsedOwnerAndRepo = parseOwnerAndRepoFromPath(normalizedPath);\n    if (!parsedOwnerAndRepo.success) {\n      return { success: false };\n    }\n\n    return {\n      success: true,\n      value: { host, ...parsedOwnerAndRepo.value },\n    };\n  } catch {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n}\n\nexport function parseRepoFromScpStyleRemoteUrl(\n  remoteUrl: string,\n): ParseResult<RepoInfo> {\n  if (remoteUrl.includes(\"://\")) {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n\n  const scpMatch = /^(?:[^@]+@)?([^:]+):(.+)$/.exec(remoteUrl);\n  if (!scpMatch) {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n\n  const host = scpMatch[1]?.trim();\n  const normalizedPath = scpMatch[2]\n    ?.trim()\n    .replace(/^\\/+/, \"\")\n    .replace(/\\.git$/i, \"\");\n  if (!host || !normalizedPath) {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n\n  const parsedOwnerAndRepo = parseOwnerAndRepoFromPath(normalizedPath);\n  if (!parsedOwnerAndRepo.success) {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n\n  return {\n    success: true,\n    value: { host, ...parsedOwnerAndRepo.value },\n  };\n}\n\nfunction parseOwnerAndRepoFromPath(\n  normalizedPath: string,\n): ParseResult<{ owner: string; repo: string }> {\n  const pathParts = normalizedPath.split(\"/\").filter((part) => part.length > 0);\n  if (pathParts.length < 2) {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n\n  const repo = pathParts[pathParts.length - 1];\n  if (!repo) {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n\n  const owner = pathParts.slice(0, -1).join(\"/\");\n  if (!owner) {\n    return { success: false };\n  }\n\n  return {\n    success: true,\n    value: { owner, repo },\n  };\n}\n","import type * as AppServer from \"app-server-types\";\n\nexport const PERSONALITY_PERSISTED_ATOM_KEY = \"composer-personality\";\n\nfunction isPersonality(value: unknown): value is AppServer.Personality {\n  return value === \"friendly\" || value === \"pragmatic\";\n}\n\nexport function parsePersonality(value: unknown): AppServer.Personality | null {\n  return isPersonality(value) ? value : null;\n}\n","import { z } from \"zod\";\n\nexport const pullRequestCommentTypeSchema = z.enum([\n  \"comment\",\n  \"review\",\n  \"review_comment\",\n]);\n\nexport const pullRequestCommentSchema = z.object({\n  authorLogin: z.string().nullable(),\n  body: z.string(),\n  createdAt: z.string(),\n  diffHunk: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  id: z.string(),\n  inReplyToId: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  line: z.number().nullable().optional(),\n  path: z.string().nullable().optional(),\n  startLine: z.number().nullable().optional(),\n  type: pullRequestCommentTypeSchema,\n  url: z.string().nullable(),\n});\n\nexport const pullRequestCommitSchema = z.object({\n  authorLogin: z.string().nullable(),\n  authorName: z.string().nullable(),\n  committedDate: z.string(),\n  messageHeadline: z.string(),\n  oid: z.string(),\n  url: z.string().nullable(),\n});\n\nexport const pullRequestTimelineSourceSchema = z.object({\n  comments: z.array(pullRequestCommentSchema),\n  commits: z.array(pullRequestCommitSchema),\n});\n\nexport type PullRequestComment = z.infer<typeof pullRequestCommentSchema>;\nexport type PullRequestCommit = z.infer<typeof pullRequestCommitSchema>;\nexport type PullRequestTimelineSource = z.infer<\n  typeof pullRequestTimelineSourceSchema\n>;\n","/** Cross-process message shapes for Codex hosts. */\nimport type * as AppServer from \"app-server-types\";\n\nimport type { Automation } from \"./automations\";\nimport type { BuildFlavor } from \"./build-flavor\";\nimport type { CommentInputItem } from \"./codex-api\";\nimport type {\n  ConfigurationKey,\n  ConfigurationKeys,\n  ConfigValueByKey,\n} from \"./configuration-keys\";\nimport type { LocalCustomAgentsResponse } from \"./custom-agents\";\nimport type {\n  CancelFetchRequest,\n  CancelFetchStreamRequest,\n  FetchRequest,\n  FetchResponse,\n  FetchStreamComplete,\n  FetchStreamError,\n  FetchStreamEvent,\n type { HostConfig, RemoteSshConnection } from \"./host-config\";\nimport type { FileDescriptor, IdeContext } from \"./ide-context\";\nimport type { AutomationArchiveReason, InboxItem } from \"./inbox\";\nimport type { LocalEnvironmentResultWithPath } from \"./local-environment\";\nimport type { CodexConfig } from \"./new-conversation\";\nimport type { AgentMode } from \"./permissions-config\";\nimport type {\n  QueuedFollowUpMessage,\n  QueuedFollowUpState,\n} from \"./queued-follow-ups\";\nimport type { RecommendedSkillsResponse } from \"./recommended-skills\";\nimport type { BuildStartConversationParamsInput } from \"./start-conversation-params\";\nimport type {\n  WorkerMessageForView,\n  WorkerMessageFromView,\n} from \"./workers/utility-types\";\n\n/**\n * Shared inbox message types to avoid magic strings across Electron/extension/webview.\n */\nexport const INBOX_MESSAGE_TYPES = {\n  CHANGED: \"inbox-items-changed\",\n} as const;\n\nexport type InboxMessageType =\n  (typeof INBOX_MESSAGE_TYPES)[keyof typeof INBOX_MESSAGE_TYPES];\n\n/**\n * Starting state for a thread not in the primary repo (cloud or worktree).\n */\nexport type AsyncThreadStartingState =\n  | {\n      type: \"working-tree\";\n    }\n  | {\n      type: \"branch\";\n      branchName: string;\n    };\n\nexport type CustomPromptTemplate = {\n  id: string;\n  description?: string | null;\n  argumentHint?: string | null;\n  content: string;\n};\n\nexport type IpcClientType = \"vscode\" | \"desktop\";\n\nexport type IpcClientConnectionStatus = \"connected\" | \"disconnected\";\nexport type TraceRecordingState =\n  | \"idle\"\n  | \"awaiting-start-confirmation\"\n  | \"recording\"\n  | \"saving\"\n  | \"awaiting-upload-details\"\n  | \"uploading\";\n\nexport type ExtendedAuthMethod = AppServer.AuthMode | \"copilot\";\nexport type ExportLogsScope = \"session\" | \"today\" | \"last7days\";\nexport type AppServerConnectionState =\n  | \"disconnected\"\n  | \"connecting\"\n  | \"unauthed\"\n  | \"connected\";\n\nexport type ImmerPatch = {\n  op: \"replace\" | \"remove\" | \"add\";\n  path: Array<string | number>;\n  value?: unknown;\n};\n\n/**\n * Streaming from one client to another happens via ThreadStreamStateChange objects.\n * The state is ConversationState from AppServerManager.\n * A snapshot is the full state object and is sent on turn start, end, and IPC client connection to ensure\n * that there is no drift over time.\n * Within a turn, patches generated from immer for _every_ modification to ConversationState are sent so other clients can
